body:
## Summary

Stacked on #27. Pairs with [self-evolving/comment-api#8](https://github.com/self-evolving/comment-api/pull/8): the drawer experience now ships from the comments service as `sepo.js`, and this PR deletes the template's vendored copy (**+85 / −1928**).

- `Comments.tsx` emits only a hidden `.sepo-embed` config element; `comments.inline.ts` shrinks to forwarding that config onto a `sepo.js` script tag from `GISCUS_APP_HOST` on each SPA navigation (sepo.js is idempotent across re-injection)
- The site's own light/dark toggle drives the widget theme via the new `window.sepoComments.setTheme()` API; `GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME` / `GISCUS_DARK_THEME` default to the service-hosted `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark` (replacing `GISCUS_THEME_URL`)
- Deleted: drawer markup/SCSS/behavior, `quartz/static/giscus/{light,dark}.css`, and the mascot webps — all served by comment-api now

Every existing `GISCUS_*` env (tabs, content repo, `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`, trigger mode) keeps working unchanged.

## Checks

- `npm run check` (tsc + prettier)
- Build with the full env (`GISCUS_APP_HOST=http://localhost:3000`, bot trigger, tabs, content repo): pages emit the `.sepo-embed` config with all attributes; `postscript.js` carries the injection logic
- Merge order note: deploy comment-api#8 before merging this, since the drawer then loads from the service

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/347GJKsqmUduDcjxQwwNXiaga1ih) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-feat-use-sepo-embed-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-feat-use-sepo-embed-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 10, 2026 7:44pm |

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 95 | PASS | 12 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | `comments.inline.ts` removes/reuses a stable `sepo-embed-script` id and registers theme cleanup on SPA nav: [lines 40-75](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L40-L75). |
| coding / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR deletes local drawer/CSS/assets and delegates drawer behavior to service `sepo.js`, leaving only config forwarding: [Comments.tsx lines 44-70](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L44-L70). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The inline script shrinks to host normalization, attribute forwarding, script injection, and theme bridging: [comments.inline.ts lines 9-75](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L9-L75). |
| coding / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Scope is limited to comments config/docs plus deletion of now-service-owned drawer assets; PR stat is focused on `README.md`, `quartz.ts`, `Comments.tsx`, `comments.inline.ts`, and removed vendored assets. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README now documents service-hosted `sepo.js`, theme env changes, and preview envs: [README lines 140-164](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/README.md#L140-L164). |
| communication / generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The README keeps the user task first: enable comments, required identifiers, then optional behavior and local testing. |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 7/7 | The env table and local Giscus test command remain directly usable: [README lines 142-174](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/README.md#L142-L174). |
| communication / generic | Keep temporary migration notes out of durable docs | pass | 6/6 | The one-time “deploy comment-api#8 first” merge-order note is in the PR body, not added to stable README docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Defaults move to service-hosted `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark`, while theme names remain configurable through `GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME` / `GISCUS_DARK_THEME`: [quartz.ts lines 145-146](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz.ts#L145-L146). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | New public config is capability-oriented (`GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME`, `GISCUS_DARK_THEME`, preview PR/branch/domain), and the existing `GISCUS_APP_HOST` continues as the service host knob. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 6/7 | Existing enums/list/numeric inputs are validated in `quartz.ts`; new `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are separate fields but only trimmed and forwarded: [lines 111-119](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz.ts#L111-L119). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | partial | 5/7 | `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR` remains numeric-validated and build output emits preview attrs, but branch/domain preview attrs have no local validation or documented service-side validation boundary. |

## Notes

- Verified locally in a detached PR worktree: `npm ci && npm run check` passed, and a full comments-enabled build emitted `.sepo-embed` with all expected attributes plus `postscript.js` injection for `sepo.js`.
- I could not inspect `self-evolving/comment-api#8` because the repository was not accessible to the available token, so service-side idempotence and `window.sepoComments.setTheme()` are scored from the PR contract plus template-side evidence.
- Smallest useful follow-up: either validate `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` / `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` locally or state that `sepo.js` owns validation for those forwarded preview fields.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are new preview-facing inputs that are passed through without local validation. This is not blocking for the template handoff, but it leaves the preview contract less explicit than `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #28 is stacked on #27 and moves the comments drawer experience out of the template into service-hosted `sepo.js`. The template now emits a hidden `.sepo-embed` config element and forwards that config onto a script tag loaded from `GISCUS_APP_HOST` on SPA navigation. It also switches default widget themes to `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark` and adds preview branch/domain metadata for the service-owned preview pill. The PR body notes that `self-evolving/comment-api#8` should deploy before this merges; that service repo was not accessible to the available token, so service-side idempotence is evaluated from the stated PR contract.

## Review
The architectural direction is sound and the diff removes a large amount of vendored drawer code in favor of a smaller template integration. There are no BLOCKING findings, but two template-side issues should be fixed or clarified before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Theme changes can be lost during sepo.js load | WARNING | The `themechange` handler only calls `window.sepoComments?.setTheme(...)`, so toggles before `sepo.js` initializes can leave the pending script with stale `data-theme`. |
| Preview branch/domain inputs need a contract boundary | WARNING | `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are trimmed and forwarded without validation or clear service-owned validation docs, unlike `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`. |
| Duplicated PR-number attributes need confirmation | INFO | `Comments.tsx` emits both `data-pr-number` and `data-preview-pr` for the same value; this may be intentional compatibility, but the consumer boundary is not explained. |

## Progress
- Current PR conversation and review artifacts were inspected, including PR #27 context.
- `check` and Vercel are passing on the current PR head.
- No existing inline comments or review threads were present before synthesis.
- Posted inline comments for `quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts:72` and `quartz.ts:119`.
- Did not resolve or minimize any existing threads or comments.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Theme changes can be lost during sepo.js load</summary>

**Cause:** [`comments.inline.ts:72`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L72) no-ops until `window.sepoComments` exists, while `mountSepo()` already snapshotted `data-theme` before appending the async script.

**Candidate solutions:** Update the pending `#sepo-embed-script` `dataset.theme` inside `onThemeChange` before calling `setTheme`, or re-apply the latest theme from a script load/init path.

**Comments:** One reviewer rated this INFO, but the current code supports the race and the fix is small.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview branch/domain inputs need a contract boundary</summary>

**Cause:** [`quartz.ts:116-119`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz.ts#L116-L119) accepts free-form preview branch/domain values and forwards them into rendered pages.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate the local contract, add a build-time warning for local-only preview-domain usage, or document that `sepo.js` owns validation and clarify in the README that `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN=localhost` is for local testing.

**Comments:** Claude and rubrics review both called out this preview-contract gap.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Duplicated PR-number attributes need confirmation</summary>

**Cause:** [`Comments.tsx:66-67`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L66-L67) emits both legacy-looking `data-pr-number` and new `data-preview-pr`.

**Candidate solutions:** Keep both with a short compatibility comment naming the consumers, or drop the unused one after confirming the `sepo.js` contract.

**Comments:** This needs service-consumer context, so it is not included as an automated action item.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved WARNING findings are concrete branch changes that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Preserve the latest theme on the pending `sepo.js` script when theme changes happen before `window.sepoComments` initializes.
- [ ] Add validation or explicit documentation for the `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` / `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` contract, including local-only `localhost` usage.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Load the drawer from the Sepo comments service (sepo.js)
